title: Pods, Multi-Container Composition, Lifecycle, Termination, and Debug Containers · API Object Lens: K8s Pod Shared Boundaries
summary: An accessible status service shares a Pod with initialization and support containers but loses requests during termination and hides its first crash. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s pod shared boundaries increment in bounded Pod composition and lifecycle manifest. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain Pod identity, scheduling unit, shared network and volumes, isolated process and filesystem boundaries, and ephemeral replacement.
Interpret deletionTimestamp and finalizers, identify the responsible controller, preserve external cleanup evidence, and repair stuck deletion safely.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Predict K8s Pod Shared Boundaries · Theory K8s Pod Shared Boundaries
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Pod has an init container that never completes, a sidecar that outlives the app, an ephemeral debug container, restart count 4, and an emptyDir holding the only diagnosis. Explain phase, container state, restart, sharing, and replacement boundaries.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the K8s Pod Shared Boundaries contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict K8s Pod Shared Boundaries · Theory K8s Pod Shared Boundaries.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for explain pod identity, scheduling unit, shared network and volumes, isolated process and filesystem boundaries, and ephemeral replacement. For Predict K8s Pod Shared Boundaries · Theory K8s Pod Shared Boundaries, keep K8s Pod Shared Boundaries connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.text: Name context, namespace, API identity, desired spec, observed status, controller authority, workload, network, storage, identity, policy, and failure-domain boundaries; predict reconciliation; review the smallest deterministic manifest change; then verify a failure, repair, rollback, and disposable-cluster transfer limit. content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example Pod has an init container that never completes, a sidecar that outlives the app, an ephemeral debug container, restart count 4, and an emptyDir holding the only diagnosis. Explain phase, container state, restart, sharing, and replacement boundaries. Competency probe: Explain Pod identity, scheduling unit, shared network and volumes, isolated process and filesystem boundaries, and ephemeral replacement. A defensible solution applies K8s Pod Shared Boundaries, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to bounded Pod composition and lifecycle manifest.

content.code:
# Worked focus: k8s-pod-shared-boundaries — Explain Pod identity, scheduling unit, shared network and volumes, isolated process and filesystem boundaries, and ephemeral replacement.
  # Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-pods-lifecycle-composition-3
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-pods-lifecycle-composition-3
    artifact: pod.yaml
    decision: worked k8s pods lifecycle composition 3
    environment: shared multi-tenant cluster under Restricted Pod Security
    fault-injection: drop one metrics series during an autoscaling surge
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked3
    change: |
      apiVersion: v1
      kind: Pod
      metadata:
      name: status-api
      spec:
      initContainers: [schema-check]
      containers: [api, telemetry-sidecar]
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 30
    verify: kubectl get pod status-api -o json && kubectl logs status-api --previous -c api
    failure: Reject durable-Pod assumptions, unbounded sidecars, swallowed signals, missing previous logs, or debug mutation presented as image repair.
    repair: Assign container ownership, bound startup and shutdown, capture states and exit causes, and prove a clean replacement preserves required behavior.
content.caption: Runnable Kubernetes object and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A Pod is a durable virtual machine and its IP, local data, and identity survive replacement. This fails the K8s Pod Shared Boundaries contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-pod-shared-boundaries-1sdirq
  - competency: k8s-pod-shared-boundaries
    artifact: pod.yaml
    decision: pod shared boundaries
    environment: high-latency edge cluster with intermittent control-plane access
    fault-injection: rotate a projected token while the API dependency slows
    retained-proof: authorization review token audience and audit correlation
    case-key: 1sdirq
    change: |
      apiVersion: v1
      kind: Pod
      metadata:
      name: status-api
      spec:
      initContainers: [schema-check]
      containers: [api, telemetry-sidecar]
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 30
    verify: kubectl get pod status-api -o json && kubectl logs status-api --previous -c api
    failure: Reject durable-Pod assumptions, unbounded sidecars, swallowed signals, missing previous logs, or debug mutation presented as image repair.
    repair: Assign container ownership, bound startup and shutdown, capture states and exit causes, and prove a clean replacement preserves required behavior.
